report_name: New 'Aur0ra' Ransomware Emerges with Stealthy Dual-Extortion Tactics

threat_actors.name: Aur0ra operators
threat_actors.description: The unnamed operators of the Aur0ra ransomware, likely operating under a Ransomware-as-a-Service (RaaS) model with affiliates, conducting opportunistic campaigns targeting various industries worldwide.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through data encryption and the threat of data leakage.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: Aur0ra ransomware encrypts files on victim systems, making them inaccessible. A key characteristic is that it does not rename the files or change their extensions, making visual identification of the damage more difficult.

attack_patterns.name: Web Protocols
attack_patterns.description: The ransomware directs victims to a Tor-based website for ransom negotiation, using web protocols over an anonymized network for command and control and communication.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service
attack_patterns.description: As part of its dual-extortion strategy, Aur0ra operators claim to have exfiltrated sensitive data before encryption, likely using common web services or cloud storage to transfer the stolen files.

attack_patterns.name: File and Directory Discovery
attack_patterns.description: Before encryption, the Aur0ra malware scans the file system to identify target files, likely prioritizing documents, images, and databases while avoiding system files to ensure the system remains operational.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: The Aur0ra ransomware is suspected of attempting to delete Volume Shadow Copies or other backups using tools like vssadmin to prevent easy restoration of encrypted files from local backups.

malware.name: Aur0ra
malware.description: A new ransomware strain that employs a dual-extortion model. It encrypts files without renaming them to evade immediate detection and drops a ransom note named '!!!README!!!DO_NOT_DELETE.txt' directing victims to a Tor negotiation portal.

tools.name: Tor
tools.description: Used by the Aur0ra operators to host the ransom negotiation portal and for anonymous communication with victims.

tools.name: vssadmin
tools.description: The vssadmin.exe utility is likely used by Aur0ra ransomware to delete Volume Shadow Copies, preventing victims from recovering encrypted files from local backups.

infrastructure.name: Aur0ra Negotiation Portal
infrastructure.description: A Tor-based website used for anonymous communication with victims for ransom negotiation and payment instructions.

indicators.name: Aur0ra Ransom Note Creation
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Aur0ra Ransomware Note Creation
id: 2b1f8c9a-7d6e-4b5a-8c3d-9e0f1a2b3c4d
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of the Aur0ra ransom note file '!!!README!!!DO_NOT_DELETE.txt'.
references:
  - https://cyfirma.com/weekly-intelligence-report-22-may-2026/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-22
tags:
  - attack.t1486
level: critical
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '\\!!!README!!!DO_NOT_DELETE.txt'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation of the ransom note file dropped by Aur0ra ransomware after encryption.

indicators.name: Aur0ra Volume Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: Volume Shadow Copy Deletion via vssadmin
id: 9a8b7c6d-5e4f-3a2b-1c0d-9e8f7a6b5c4d
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common ransomware technique to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://cyfirma.com/weekly-intelligence-report-22-may-2026/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-22
tags:
  - attack.t1490
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the command used to delete Volume Shadow Copies, a technique associated with Aur0ra to prevent system recovery.

indicators.name: Tor Client Execution for C2
indicators.pattern:
title: Tor Client Execution
id: 1a2b3c4d-5e6f-7a8b-9c0d-1e2f3a4b5c6d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of the Tor client (tor.exe), which may indicate C2 communications for ransomware like Aur0ra, especially on servers where it is not expected.
references:
  - https://cyfirma.com/weekly-intelligence-report-22-may-2026/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-22
tags:
  - attack.t1071.001
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\tor.exe'
  filter_legit:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\Tor Browser\Browser\firefox.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter_legit
indicators.description: Detects the execution of the Tor client, which Aur0ra uses for anonymous C2 communications.
